title: convolvulus bidrensis
intro: Convolvulus bidrensis, also known as the Bidens convolvulus or two-headed beggarticks, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Convolvulus bidrensis include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as the treatment of wounds and injuries. Convolvulus bidrensis can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
